Comments: There is no explanation regarding the unavailability of subtitles for the movie *The Sixth Sense*, and if such an explanation is unavailable, I believe it is only proper not to include movies requiring subtitles in the purchase options in the first place. It is an ironic situation where movies with subtitles are priced at 6,000 won or less, while those without are priced at 6,000 won or more. Of course, this is a matter of consumer choice and not an issue in itself, but for the sake of improvement, the first step should be to make the presence or absence of subtitles more clearly visible. Secondly, I believe it is desirable to price movies without subtitles. I hope that if subtitles become available in the future, consumers will be notified of their right to make a decision based on their choice, so that we can expect better quality from YouTube movies.
root_cause: Lack of transparency regarding subtitle availability and inconsistent pricing for non-subtitled content
